reasoning: Both models skipped the only critical file (PKGBUILD) due to false binary detection, leaving zero files audited. Without reviewing PKGBUILD—which defines build/install logic—no safety assessment is possible.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD (critical build script) was skipped by both models due to erroneous 'binary file' classification
false_negatives: All potential risks in PKGBUILD (e.g., unsafe makepkg hooks, malicious commands) were missed due to skipped review
learnings: Improve file type detection: PKGBUILD must never be misclassified as binary. Implement MIME-type/hex-check fallbacks.
Prioritization failure: Critical files like PKGBUILD should halt audit if unreadable rather than skipping silently.
Add validation for file-reading step to catch encoding/binary false positives before model invocation.
